summaryText: Pastor Travis Hearn opened the service by reading 2 Chronicles 7:14, presenting it as a four-point blueprint for revival. He declared that the current state of moral darkness and national depression makes the world ripe for a spiritual awakening, which always begins with one consecrated person. The message centered on the four ingredients from the scripture: humility, prayer, seeking God's face, and turning from wicked ways. Pastor Travis passionately testified that Impact Church is already experiencing a powerful revival, evidenced by consistent large-scale baptisms, packed services, a thriving young adults ministry, and student-led movements in local schools. He challenged the congregation to move beyond a 'spiritual roller coaster' of inconsistency and to personally say 'yes' to God, emphasizing that revival starts with individual surrender. The sermon concluded with a call for personal repentance and a public invitation to surrender to Jesus Christ, resulting in many raising their hands in response.
mainPoints: Revival begins with personal humility, recognizing our complete dependence on God and moving from a heart of entitlement to one of gratitude.
Revival is fueled by prayer and seeking God's intimate presence (His face), not just His blessings (His hand).
Revival requires turning from wicked ways through genuine repentance, which involves confessing sin and forsaking it to walk in the light.

versesMentioned.text: If my people, who are called by my name, will humble themselves and pray and seek my face and turn from their wicked ways, then I will hear from heaven, and I will forgive their sin and will heal their land.
As for you, you were dead in your transgressions and sins,
Who dares despise the day of small things, since the seven eyes of the LORD that range throughout the earth will rejoice when they see the chosen capstone in the hand of Zerubbabel?'

Jabez cried out to the God of Israel, 'Oh, that you would bless me and enlarge my territory! Let your hand be with me, and keep me from harm so that I will be free from pain.' And God granted his request.
Don't let anyone look down on you because you are young, but set an example for the believers in speech, in conduct, in love, in faith and in purity.
You will seek me and find me when you seek me with all your heart.
Ask and it will be given to you; seek and you will find; knock and the door will be opened to you.

Yet I hold this against you: You have forsaken the love you had at first. Consider how far you have fallen! Repent and do the things you did at first. If you do not repent, I will come to you and remove your lampstand from its place.
Peter replied, 'Repent and be baptized, every one of you, in the name of Jesus Christ for the forgiveness of your sins. And you will receive the gift of the Holy Spirit.'
If we confess our sins, he is faithful and just and will forgive us our sins and purify us from all unrighteousness.
If you declare with your mouth, 'Jesus is Lord,' and believe in your heart that God raised him from the dead, you will be saved.

Monday.scripture: 2 Chronicles 7:14 (NIV) - 'If my people, who are called by my name, will humble themselves and pray...'
Monday.action: Begin your day by kneeling in prayer, acknowledging your complete dependence on God for everything.
Monday.prayer: Lord, I humble myself before you today. I am nothing without you. Help me to live with a heart of gratitude, not entitlement.

Tuesday.scripture: 1 Chronicles 4:10 (NIV) - 'Jabez cried out to the God of Israel, "Oh, that you would bless me and enlarge my territory!"'
Tuesday.action: Set aside 10 minutes to pray specifically for revival—in your own heart, your family, your church, and your nation.
Tuesday.prayer: God, like Jabez, I ask you to enlarge my territory for your glory. Bring revival to every area of my influence.

Wednesday.scripture: Jeremiah 29:13 (NIV) - 'You will seek me and find me when you seek me with all your heart.'
Wednesday.action: In your quiet time, focus worship solely on who God is (His character), not on what you need from Him.
Wednesday.prayer: Father, I seek your face today, not just your hand. I desire intimacy with you above all blessings.

Thursday.scripture: 1 John 1:9 (NIV) - 'If we confess our sins, he is faithful and just and will forgive us our sins...'
Thursday.action: Identify one attitude or behavior that does not please God. Confess it specifically and ask for His strength to turn from it.
Thursday.prayer: Lord, I confess my sin of [name it]. Thank you for your faithfulness to forgive. Purify me and help me walk in your light.

Friday.scripture: Zechariah 4:10 (NIV) - 'Who dares despise the day of small things...'
Friday.action: Take one small, faithful step in an area God has been prompting you (e.g., a conversation, a act of service, starting a Bible study).
Friday.prayer: God, I will not despise small beginnings. I rejoice to start this work for you. Use my small 'yes' for your great purpose.

Saturday.scripture: Romans 10:9 (NIV) - 'If you declare with your mouth, "Jesus is Lord," and believe in your heart... you will be saved.'
Saturday.action: Reflect on your salvation. If you have never fully surrendered, declare Jesus as Lord today. If you have, reaffirm your commitment out loud.
Saturday.prayer: Jesus, I declare with my mouth that you are Lord. I believe you rose from the dead. I surrender my life to you completely today.
